perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party agrees that receipt of Confidential Information, pursuant to this Agreement, is exclusively for the purpose of evaluating a potential acquisition of <PAGE> MyPoints.com and Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Client agrees that receipt of Confidential Information, pursuant to this Agreement, is exclusively for the purpose of evaluating a potential partnership of <PAGE> MyPoints.com and Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Receiving Party' remains, but 'acquisition' is changed to 'partnership' and 'Receiving Party' is added in again which creates a direct contradiction of what they can use the info for. Is it partnership or aquisition? This contradicts the usage of the information by either Receiving Party or Client. The phrase that includes 'Receiving Party' is still in the same sentance, creating a clear in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party agrees that receipt of Confidential Information, pursuant to this Agreement, is exclusively for the purpose of evaluating a potential partnership of <PAGE> MyPoints.com and Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to a 'potential acquisition' whereas the changed text alters this to a 'potential partnership' while still using the phrase 'Receiving Party' in the same sentence. The contradicted text also states 'potential partnership', but the fact that the original and changed text use both Receiving Party and partnership/acquisition in the same sentence, creates an in-text contradiction, because the same party cannot be both 'Receiving Party' and 'Client', as well as both 'acquisition' and 'partnership'. This change in terminology, while maintaining the same phrasing creates confusion and a clear contradiction, especially because the contract defines what each party is allowed to use the confidential information for based on either 'partnership' or 'acquisition'. Therefore, based on the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, this is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party hereunder shall: a. not be copied or distributed, disclosed, or disseminated in any way or form by the Receiving Party to any third party without the written permission of MyPoints.com first obtained; b. be treated by the Receiving Party with the same degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party as is used with respect to the Receiving Party's own proprietary and confidential information of like importance; c. remain the property of the MyPoints.com, and shall be returned by the Receiving Party to MyPoints.com (along with all copies thereof) promptly upon its receipt of a request from MyPoints.com to do so; d. not be used by Receiving Party for any purpose other than as specified herein or otherwise approved by MyPoints.com in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party hereunder shall: a. not be copied or distributed, disclosed, or disseminated in any way or form by the Client to any third party without the written permission of MyPoints.com first obtained; b. be treated by the Customer with the same degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party as is used with respect to the Receiving Party's own proprietary and confidential information of like importance; c. remain the property of the MyPoints.com, and shall be returned by the Receiving Party to MyPoints.com (along with all copies thereof) promptly upon its receipt of a request from MyPoints.com to do so; d. not be used by Customer for any purpose other than as specified herein or otherwise approved by MyPoints.com in writing.
perturbation.explanation: Introducing 'Client' and 'Customer' into the obligations section where 'Receiving Party' is also referenced creates confusion. Who is responsible for what actions? 'Receiving Party','Client', or 'Customer'? This leads to a contradiction regarding duties. This affects the whole document in terms of responsibilities for confidential information. This is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party hereunder shall: a. not be copied or distributed, disclosed, or disseminated in any way or form by the Client to any third party without the written permission of MyPoints.com first obtained; b. be treated by the Customer with the same degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party as is used with respect to the Receiving Party's own proprietary and confidential information of like importance; c. remain the property of the MyPoints.com, and shall be returned by the Receiving Party to MyPoints.com (along with all copies thereof) promptly upon its receipt of a request from MyPoints.com to do so; d. not be used by Customer for any purpose other than as specified herein or otherwise approved by MyPoints.com in writing.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation introduces 'Client' and 'Customer' in addition to the existing 'Receiving Party' within the same clause outlining obligations regarding Confidential Information. This creates ambiguity and confusion about who is responsible for specific actions, as it's unclear whether the obligations apply to all three terms or only to specific ones. This directly contradicts the clarity needed for enforcement, as the Contradicted Text (which is identical to the changed text in this case) now contains conflicting designations, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations set forth in Paragraph 3 above shall not apply to any information which: a. is already in the public domain at the time of disclosure to the Receiving Party or becomes available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; b. was lawfully in the Receiving Party's possession prior to receipt from the MyPoints.com; c. is disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party with the right to do so. For the purposes of this Paragraph 4, information shall not be deemed to be in the public domain merely because any part of said information is embodied in general disclosures or because individual features, components or combinations thereof are now, or become, known to the public, provided, however, that the obligations of Paragraph 3 hereof shall not apply to any such part of said information.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations set forth in Paragraph 3 above shall not apply to any information which: a. is already in the public domain at the time of disclosure to the Client or becomes available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by the Customer; b. was lawfully in the Partner's possession prior to receipt from the MyPoints.com; c. is disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party with the right to do so. For the purposes of this Paragraph 4, information shall not be deemed to be in the public domain merely because any part of said information is embodied in general disclosures or because individual features, components or combinations thereof are now, or become, known to the public, provided, however, that the obligations of Paragraph 3 hereof shall not apply to any such part of said information.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'Receiving Party' is replaced by 'Client', 'Customer', and 'Partner' in different parts of the sentence. This makes it ambiguous who is responsible for the exceptions related to publicly available information, information already possessed, or information received from a third party. This directly contradicts with the rest of the document regarding the obligations of 'Receiving Party' in Paragraph 3 as mentioned in the main sentence. This in-text contradiction causes confusion regarding the obligations of each party, especially since Paragraph 3 now mentions 'Client', 'Customer', and 'Receiving Party' when discussing obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations set forth in Paragraph 3 above shall not apply to any information which: a. is already in the public domain at the time of disclosure to the Client or becomes available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by the Customer; b. was lawfully in the Partner's possession prior to receipt from the MyPoints.com; c. is disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party with the right to do so. For the purposes of this Paragraph 4, information shall not be deemed to be in the public domain merely because any part of said information is embodied in general disclosures or because individual features, components or combinations thereof are now, or become, known to the public, provided, however, that the obligations of Paragraph 3 hereof shall not apply to any such part of said information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Client', 'Customer', and 'Partner' in the same sentence. This creates ambiguity and conflicts with the contradicted section which outlines the obligations of the 'Receiving Party'. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the contract now uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, causing a strong in-text contradiction.
